documents.title: Guidelines for Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic Work

ai_strategy.summary: GW publishes administrative AI guidance, academic generative AI guidelines, privacy guidance, and an IT page for approved enterprise AI tools. The posture is governance-first: responsible procurement, privacy, data classification, and faculty-level academic expectations are the strongest public signals.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: George Washington University's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

bodies.name: GW Information Technology
bodies.scope: Reviews AI tools, procurement, and enterprise AI services.

bodies.name: GW Privacy Office
bodies.scope: Publishes privacy guidance for use of artificial intelligence.

governance_structure.summary: George Washington University's public AI governance is organized through GW Information Technology, GW Privacy Office.

initiatives.title: Privacy guidance for artificial intelligence

initiatives.title: Pegasus GPU and Grace Hopper HPC expansion
initiatives.description: GW is expanding Pegasus with state-of-the-art GPU-based systems including Nvidia Grace Hopper superchip and L40S GPU resources, building on a Pegasus cluster with V100 GPU nodes and more than 2 PFLOPs of single-precision capacity.
initiatives.significance: This closes GW’s compute-capacity gap with a named cluster and public GPU configuration.

research_centers.name: Institute for Data, Democracy & Politics
research_centers.focus: Research on data, democracy, politics, and AI governance/policy implications.

research_centers.name: Digital Trade and Data Governance Hub
research_centers.focus: Data governance, AI policy, and digital trade research.

computing_infrastructure.summary: GW’s Pegasus flagship HPC cluster has 200+ nodes and 2.14 PFLOPs single-precision capacity. Public specifications list 16 small GPU nodes with two NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs each and 22 large GPU nodes with four V100 SXM2 GPUs each, plus an expansion adding Nvidia Grace Hopper superchip and L40S GPU systems for advanced AI, CFD, and large-memory workloads; the recorded count reflects the 120 V100 GPUs implied by the published node table.

student_facing_ai.name: Generative AI academic-work guidelines
student_facing_ai.scope: Students and faculty using generative AI in coursework.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Privacy and walled-garden AI guidance
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: GW privacy guidance recommends walled-garden regimes and role-based access for AI tools handling university data.

regulatory_context.summary: George Washington University operates in District of Columbia; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and institutional privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: George Washington University now has stronger public evidence for both platforms and compute. GW IT lists Microsoft Copilot, GW Chat, Box AI, Zoom AI, Google Gemini, and NotebookLM statuses, with Copilot available to faculty, students, and staff for public and restricted data. Research compute is anchored in Pegasus, a 200+ node flagship HPC cluster with a published V100 GPU configuration and an expansion adding Nvidia Grace Hopper and L40S GPU systems for advanced AI and computational workloads. Public gaps remain around contract values, active-user counts, AI-specific budget totals, and subgroup student outcomes.

gaps_acknowledged: No GW chief AI officer or single AI strategy office was found.
GW publishes approved AI tool status and Pegasus GPU capacity, but enterprise tool user counts, contract values, and comprehensive AI budget totals were not disclosed.
No student subgroup outcome metrics tied to AI tools or teaching guidance were found.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: platform first.
